subject: 📊 Daily Portfolio: Markets closed | VERA PDUFA Monday — July 7
html_body:
Saturday · July 4, 2026 · Kuwait 09:00
📊 Daily Portfolio
🇺🇸 NYSE CLOSED — US Independence Day (weekend observance)
Last prices from Thursday Jul 2 close. Next open: Monday July 7 — same day as VERA PDUFA.
Est. Portfolio
$767
Jul 2 close
🎯 Withdrawal goal: ~$767 / $1,000 — $233 to go (76.7%)
VERA approval alone could close most of this gap. CRL wipes ~$150.
🚨 BINARY EVENT — MONDAY MARKET OPEN
VERA — Atacicept PDUFA
July 7, 2026 · FDA decision on IgA Nephropathy BLA
Days
3
to decision
Position
6 shares @ $42
Cost: $252.00
Jul 2 Close

Approval Prob.
89%
14 analysts Strong Buy, avg $78
✅ APPROVAL (+50 to +100%)
Est. price: $60–80
TP1 @ $55: Sell 3 shares
Gross: +$78 | Net: +$75 (−$3 commission)
TP2 @ $78: Hold remaining 3
First B-cell modulator (BAFF+APRIL) — differentiated MOA vs Filspari/Tarpeyo
💀 CRL / REJECTION (−70 to −80%)
Est. price: $10–15
Exit all 6 shares at open
Net loss: ~−$155 to −$165
(after $3 exit commission)
Don't hold through a CRL — proteinuria endpoint rejection = thesis broken
⚡ Pre-decision brief
FDA decisions typically arrive before 9:30 AM ET — check IBKR or the FDA website on Monday morning before the open. Don't use a market order at 9:30; wait for the first 15-minute candle to settle and let institutional flow define the real level, then decide trim vs hold. The IgAN space is competitive (Tarpeyo, Filspari, Povetacicept PDUFA Nov 2026), but atacicept's dual BAFF+APRIL mechanism is the first of its kind — the label differentiation matters for prescription patterns.
📋 Why the 89% is credible
NEJM Phase 3: proteinuria −46% vs placebo. Priority Review + Accelerated Approval pathway. FDA aligned with Vera on revised eGFR analysis plan (proactive regulator engagement = strong signal). Historical Priority Review BLA approval rate: ~87%. Atacicept is above base rate due to Phase 3 quality and FDA alignment.

First check on Monday Jul 7 open — if holding above $135, the thesis is alive. If VERA approval drives risk-on sentiment Monday morning, ANET may recover toward $150+ before Q2 earnings risk in early August. Exit by July 25 regardless.
TP1: $185 (full exit) · Stop: $135 · Deadline: July 25 · Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — check Musaffa before adding
⏳ Pending Watchlist
CAPRCapricor Therapeutics 🟡 SOON · 25 days to AdCom
🔒 Gate: Enter only after VERA July 7 result. Capital decision pending.
Price Jul 2
$24.84
✅ Below $26 target
Down −5.9% Jul 2
Entry: $26.00 · Stop: $18.00 · TP1: $52.00
AdCom: July 29 · PDUFA: August 22, 2026
Drug: Deramiocel (CAP-1002) · DMD cardiomyopathy
Phase 3 HOPE-3: hit primary endpoint (PUL v2.0) and LVEF secondary with statistical significance. Historical AdCom vote for DMD drugs: +50–100% on positive. Advisory committee reviews July 29 before Aug 22 PDUFA.
⚠️ Smart money caution:
Unusual bearish put activity Jun 30 — 5:1 put/call ratio, 2900-contract Aug 21 $20 put block. Monitor options flow before entering. Real red flag until explained.
Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — check Musaffa. Conviction: 6/10. Target: $150 position.
→ Plan: Post-VERA (July 7): enter ~$150 at $24–26. Stop $18. AdCom July 29 = catalyst. Exit pre-PDUFA if AdCom vote is negative.
Archived: VRDN (Lumvoa approved Jun 30, entry missed) · VERA watchlist entry (now active position)
📡 Catalyst Plays — July 4, 2026
15-search sweep completed. FDA calendar doesn't pause for holidays. Honest screen: one genuine small-cap find today.
TENXTenax Therapeutics, Inc. WATCH · 3/5
Catalyst: Phase 3 LEVEL data release — August 2026~25–55 days away
🔥 FRESH — Jul 2 (yesterday): ESC Congress 2026 awarded TENX a Late-Breaking Clinical Science slot (Aug 28–31 Munich). Highest-tier academic signal — independent peer review of trial design before public data release. This slot is awarded only when the data is expected to be significant.
Drug: TNX-103 (oral levosimendan) for PH-HFpEF (pulmonary hypertension in heart failure with preserved ejection fraction)
Market cap: ~$386M · Small float
Trial: N=230+, powered >90% to detect 25m 6MWD improvement. BSSR confirmed Dec 2025.
Phase 3 success probability: ~55–65% (HFpEF base rate; >90% power is technically strong)
✅ Positive
+100 to +300%
PH-HFpEF large unmet need, no effective oral drug. Small cap + Phase 3 win = explosive.
💀 Negative
−50 to −80%
HFpEF trials have historically underperformed mechanistic hypothesis.
EV on 30% position (~$230): 60% × +$460 − 40% × −$115 = +$230 theoretical EV
Smart money: ESC Late-Breaking slot = strongest pre-data independent signal
Cash runway: ~$50M — potential dilution risk. Verify before entry.
Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — check Musaffa.
August data gives time to enter post-VERA (July 7), ~3–4 weeks to build position before ESC. Say "analyze TENX" for full deep dive.
CELCCelcuitySKIP
PDUFA July 17 for gedatolisib (breast cancer) — 13 days away. But market cap expanded from ~$1.7B to $5.18B as stock ran $12 → $106 into the PDUFA. Fails Gate 2 (≥$2B). The crowd got here months ago. Lesson: PDUFA plays work best when you're early.
KPTIKaryopharmSKIP
Phase 3 XPORT-EC-042 endometrial cancer data expected "mid-2026" — vague, may have passed. Fails Gate 1 (no confirmed date within 45 days). Revenue base also limits pure binary upside. Check news for any data release.
💵 Cash Deployment — $352.56 Available
⏸ HOLD — wait for VERA resolution July 7
Binary event in 3 days that can swing −$150 to +$120+. Deploying now adds risk on top of risk.
If VERA approved:
Portfolio jumps. Deploy toward CAPR (~$150 at $24–26, AdCom Jul 29) or begin building TENX before August data.
If VERA rejected:
Cash is your only clean capital. Hold — don't compound a loss with a new entry.
Math when ready: $352.56 − $6 reserve − $3 commission = $343.56 net deployable. CAPR at ~$25: 13 shares ≈ $325 (within $150 target). Your call.
📚 Today's Concept — Day 16
Earnings Whisper Numbers — and why FDA decisions work the same way
Sellside analysts publish a consensus EPS estimate — that's the "official" expectation. The buyside whisper is what institutions actually expect, usually higher and based on channel checks. The stock reacts to the gap between the actual result and the whisper, not the consensus. NVDA beating consensus by $0.05 while the whisper was a $0.12 beat = stock drops on a "beat." FDA decisions work identically. The published 89% approval probability for VERA atacicept is the consensus. The buyside whisper lives in the options market — VERA's straddle pricing implies a ±50–60% move. If you paid $42 and the market is pricing in a $24 swing, the "upside" on approval to $64+ is already the consensus scenario, not a surprise. The edge — and the risk — is in what happens when the result differs from what's implied. A surprise approval with unusually strong label breadth could drive the stock past the implied move. A CRL that nobody hedged for is where the real pain hides. Watch the post-decision move: does VERA overshoot the implied range? That tells you whether the buyside whisper was more conservative than $40 prices suggest.
Also today: Approval base rates — FDA approves ~85% of applications with Priority Review; biologics (like atacicept) run ~87%. The 89% for VERA is above base rate — earned by Phase 3 data quality and FDA alignment on the eGFR analysis plan. Base rate is your starting prior; modifiers move it from there.
Research only — not financial advice. All positions subject to risk of total loss.
